name: Go Low Indoor Golf
about: Go Low Indoor Golf is an indoor golf facility featuring five state-of-the-art simulators where guests can play on virtual courses, practice at the driving range, or enjoy interactive games. The venue offers a range of services including leagues, events, and personalized golf lessons led by instructor Gary Samborgna. It caters to all skill levels with its aboutGOLF launch monitors, which are noted for their accuracy. Pricing varies depending on the service, and the facility is available for both individual sessions and private events.
why_golfers_like_it: State-of-the-art aboutGOLF launch monitors with high accuracy (within 5 yards)
Versatile facility capable of hosting parties, leagues, and corporate events
Extensive library of over 2,000 virtual golf courses
Ability to bring your own food and drinks for events
On-site golf instruction available for all skill levels

food_and_drink.notes: Guests can bring their own food. Snacks are also available for purchase.
Guests can bring their own alcoholic beverages.

simulator_hardware.notes: aboutGOLF launch monitors used for simulation

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: No, you don't need to bring your own clubs. The facility provides clubs for borrowing, but it's recommended to let them know in advance if you need a set reserved for you.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.answer: Yes, you can bring your own food and drinks, including alcohol. The facility also provides snacks and non-alcoholic beverages for purchase.

faq.question: How long does it typically take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: It typically takes 1 hour per player to complete an 18-hole round. If it's your first time using a simulator, it may take a little longer.

faq.question: Is the venue kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, Go Low Indoor Golf is kid-friendly. It offers a junior golf program and features various mini-games and activities suitable for all skill levels, including children.
